randomNumber:
# Generate a random number

# Generate a random integer within an inclusive range
min = 1
max = 100
random_int = rand(min..max)
puts "Random Integer [#{min}-#{max}]: #{random_int}"

# Generate a random float between 0.0 and 1.0
random_float = rand(Float64)
puts "Random Float [0.0-1.0): #{random_float}"

# Select a random element from an array
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
if random_fruit = fruits.sample?
  puts "Random fruit: #{random_fruit}"
end

# Select multiple unique random elements
if two_fruits = fruits.sample?(2)
  puts "Two random fruits: #{two_fruits.join(", ")}"
end
reverseString:
# Reverses a string

original = "Hello, Crystal!"

# The .reverse method on String handles Unicode correctly
reversed = original.reverse

puts "Original: #{original}"
puts "Reversed: #{reversed}"

# Unicode example
original_unicode = "你好 Crystal"
puts "Original Unicode: #{original_unicode}"
puts "Reversed Unicode: #{original_unicode.reverse}"
fibonacci:
# Generates a Fibonacci sequence

# Iterative function to calculate the Nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed)
# Returns Int64. Use BigInt for numbers beyond the Int64 limit.
def fibonacci(n : Int)
  raise ArgumentError.new("Input must be non-negative") if n < 0
  return 0_i64 if n == 0
  return 1_i64 if n == 1

  a, b = 0_i64, 1_i64
  (n - 1).times do
    a, b = b, a + b
  end
  b
end

num_terms = 15
sequence = (0...num_terms).map { |i| fibonacci(i) }

puts "First #{num_terms} Fibonacci numbers:"
pp sequence

# Int64 can handle up to F(92)
puts "F(90) = #{fibonacci(90)}"
factorial:
# Calculates the factorial of a number

# Iterative function to calculate factorial N!
# Returns Int64. Use BigInt for numbers beyond the Int64 limit (n > 20).
def factorial(n : Int)
  raise ArgumentError.new("Factorial is not defined for negative numbers") if n < 0
  return 1_i64 if n == 0
  
  # Use inject to multiply all numbers in the range
  (1..n).map(&.to_i64).product
end

num = 5
puts "Factorial of #{num} is #{factorial(num)}"

num_zero = 0
puts "Factorial of #{num_zero} is #{factorial(num_zero)}"

# Int64 overflows after 20!
num_large = 20
puts "Factorial of #{num_large} is #{factorial(num_large)}"
primeCheck:
# Checks if a number is prime

def is_prime?(n : Int64)
  return false if n <= 1
  return true if n <= 3
  return false if n % 2 == 0 || n % 3 == 0

  limit = Math.sqrt(n).to_i64
  i = 5_i64
  while i <= limit
    return false if n % i == 0 || n % (i + 2) == 0
    i += 6
  end
  true
end

numbers_to_check = [2_i64, 3_i64, 4_i64, 17_i64, 25_i64, 29_i64, 97_i64, 104729_i64]

numbers_to_check.each do |num|
  if is_prime?(num)
    puts "#{num} is prime."
  else
    puts "#{num} is not prime."
  end
end
readFile:
require "file"

filename = "crystal_read_test.txt"

# Create a dummy file for reading
begin
  File.write(filename, "First line from Crystal.\nSecond line.\n")
  puts "Created dummy file: #{filename}"
rescue ex
  puts "Error creating dummy file: #{ex.message}"
  exit
end

puts "\n--- Reading file '#{filename}' ---"
if File.exists?(filename)
  begin
    # Method 1: Read all lines into an array
    lines = File.read_lines(filename)
    puts "Content read line by line:"
    lines.each_with_index do |line, i|
      puts "Line #{i + 1}: #{line}"
    end

    # Method 2: Read entire file into a single string
    # content = File.read(filename)
    # puts "\nEntire file content:\n#{content}"

  rescue ex
    puts "Error reading file: #{ex.message}"
  end
else
  puts "Error: File not found."
end
writeFile:
require "file"

filename = "output_crystal.txt"

# 1. Overwrite or create a new file
puts "--- Writing (Overwrite) to '#{filename}' ---"
try
  content = "This line overwrites existing content.\nWritten from Crystal.\n"
  File.write(filename, content)
  puts "Content written successfully."
rescue ex
  puts "Error writing to file: #{ex.message}"
end

# 2. Append to the existing file
puts "\n--- Appending to '#{filename}' ---"
try
  content_to_append = "This line was appended.\n"
  File.append(filename, content_to_append)
  puts "Content appended successfully."
rescue ex
  puts "Error appending to file: #{ex.message}"
end
fileExists:
require "file"
require "dir"

file_to_check = "crystal.json" # Should exist
dir_to_check = "."             # Current directory
non_existent = "no_such_file.cr"

puts "--- Checking for files and directories ---"

# Check if a path exists and is a file
puts "'#{file_to_check}' is a file? #{File.file?(file_to_check)}"
puts "'#{dir_to_check}' is a file? #{File.file?(dir_to_check)}"

# Check if a path exists and is a directory
puts "'#{dir_to_check}' is a directory? #{Dir.exists?(dir_to_check)}"
puts "'#{file_to_check}' is a directory? #{Dir.exists?(file_to_check)}"

# Check if a path exists (either file or directory)
puts "'#{non_existent}' exists? #{File.exists?(non_existent) || Dir.exists?(non_existent)}"
dateTime:
require "time"

# Get current time in the local timezone
local_now = Time.local
puts "Current Local Time: #{local_now}"

# Get current time in UTC
utc_now = Time.utc
puts "Current UTC Time:   #{utc_now}"

puts "\n--- Components ---"
puts "Year: #{local_now.year}, Month: #{local_now.month}, Day: #{local_now.day}"
puts "Hour: #{local_now.hour}, Minute: #{local_now.minute}, Second: #{local_now.second}"
puts "Timezone: #{local_now.zone}"

puts "\n--- Formatting (using strftime) ---"
# See Crystal docs for format codes (similar to C/Ruby)
puts "Custom format (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS): #{local_now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")}"
puts "Readable format: #{local_now.strftime("%A, %B %-d, %Y")}"

puts "\n--- Epoch Time ---"
puts "Seconds since epoch: #{local_now.to_unix}"
puts "Milliseconds since epoch: #{local_now.to_unix_ms}"
maxInArray:
# Finds the maximum value in an array

numbers = [10, 5, 42, 17, 8, 99, 32, -5, 0]
puts "Array: #{numbers}"

# The .max method returns the maximum element.
# It returns `nil` for an empty array, so the result type is a union (e.g., Int32 | Nil)
if max_value = numbers.max
  puts "Maximum value: #{max_value}"
else
  puts "The array is empty."
end

# Example with an empty array
empty_array = [] of Int32
puts "\nArray: #{empty_array}"
if max_value = empty_array.max
  puts "Maximum value: #{max_value}"
else
  puts "The array is empty."
end
